headline: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Stock After Recent Pullback Is The Price Now Fair?
summary: For anyone watching Palantir Technologies and wondering whether the current price reflects fair value or hype, this article focuses on what the numbers actually say about the stock. Recently, Palantir Technologies has seen the share price close at US$128.47, with returns that declined 4.6% over the last week, 6.1% over the last month, and 23.5% year to date, while the return over three years has been very large and the five year return is more than 3x the starting point. News flow around...

headline: Palantir Secures Foundational Role in NGC2 Data Layer
summary: MIAMI, June 22, 2026--Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), a global leader in artificial intelligence and data platforms, today welcomed the U.S. Army’s announcement that it has established the Next Generation Command and Control common data layer baseline. Palantir is proud to support the Army, its commanders, and Soldiers as they modernize command and control with a data-driven approach built to scale across the force.

content.title: Palantir Secures Foundational Role in NGC2 Data Layer
content.summary: MIAMI, June 22, 2026--Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), a global leader in artificial intelligence and data platforms, today welcomed the U.S. Army’s announcement that it has established the Next Generation Command and Control common data layer baseline. Palantir is proud to support the Army, its commanders, and Soldiers as they modernize command and control with a data-driven approach built to scale across the force.

headline: Nasdaq Drops 1.3%, Dow Weathers the Mag 7 Storm
summary: A selloff of large technology stocks sent the Nasdaq reeling, but the Dow managed to weather the storm. The Dow rose 163 points, or 0.3%. The Nasdaq fell 1.3%. The S&P 500 fell 0.4%, even though breadth was solid for much of the afternoon.

content.title: Nasdaq Drops 1.3%, Dow Weathers the Mag 7 Storm
content.summary: A selloff of large technology stocks sent the Nasdaq reeling, but the Dow managed to weather the storm. The Dow rose 163 points, or 0.3%. The Nasdaq fell 1.3%. The S&P 500 fell 0.4%, even though breadth was solid for much of the afternoon.
